title: Pleasantly Poached Croatian Vegetable Bowl
description: A winey, silky solo-dinner featuring vegetable stock and kombu-cold-extract with a focus on kosher-friendly ingredients.
instructions: In a large pot, combine vegetable stock, carrots, celery, and cabbage.
Bring to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to medium-low and simmer for 20 minutes.
Strain the stock through a fine-mesh sieve into a large bowl, discarding the solids.
Stir in the kombu cold-extract, kosher salt, and black pepper.
Heat the olive oil in a small skillet over medium heat.
Add the red wine vinegar to the skillet and stir to combine.
Pour the hot oil and vinegar mixture over the vegetable mixture and stir to combine.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed.
Serve the poached vegetables hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley.
Arrange the vegetables on a wooden board and serve immediately.
Drizzle any remaining dressing over the top of the vegetables and serve.
